key_insight: PINNSur effectively uses physics-informed neural networks for solving PDEs on curved surfaces, providing an alternative to the finite element method that addresses mesh quality issues.
engineering_relevance: This framework enhances geometry processing applications by enabling effective solutions for partial differential equations on various surface topologies.
method: Trains a neural field to approximate surface normals.
Expresses differential operators via projections from R^3 onto the surface.
Covers all orientable manifolds, regardless of curvature.
evidence_signal: The research investigates the convergence behavior of PINNs empirically, addressing their lack of convergence guarantees.
watch_item: The challenge of achieving convergence may arise not only from function approximation but also from the geometric approximation of the surface.
paper_title: PINNsur: Physics-Informed Neural Networks for PDEs on Curved Surfaces
